ising1d_analysis.notes: No phase transition in 1D. Free energy f=-kT·ln(λ₊) is EML-2 everywhere. λ₊ involves sqrt(sinh²+exp) → EML-2. ln(λ₊) adds depth → EML-2. Globally analytic, no EML-inf singularity.

ising2d_analysis.notes: T_c = 2J/(k_B·ln(1+√2)) ≈ 2.2692. Free energy analytic away from T_c (EML-2). Onsager logarithm |T-T_c|²·ln|T-T_c| at T_c → EML-inf. Heat capacity diverges logarithmically: exactly the EML-inf signature.

einstein_analysis.notes: All quantities rational in exp(-βℏω): EML-2. C_V → 3Nk_B at high T (Dulong-Petit), → 0 exponentially at low T. No kinks, no singularities: global EML-2.

debye_analysis.t3_law: C_V ∝ T³ at low T: polynomial, EML-2
debye_analysis.debye_integral: D(x) = (3/x³)∫ t³/(e^t-1): no elementary closed form → transcendental (EML-inf numerically, EML-2 via series approx)
debye_analysis.notes: T³ law at low T is EML-2. High-T Dulong-Petit limit is EML-0. Full Debye function needs integral → computationally EML-2 via series. No phase transitions.

ideal_gas_analysis.notes: Thermal de Broglie wavelength Λ∝T^{-1/2}: EML-2 (sqrt). Free energy and entropy: EML-2 (ln·T). Pressure P=NkT/V: EML-1 (linear in T). No phase transitions. Global EML-2 except for pressure (EML-1).

vdw_analysis.transition_order: 1st order (liquid-gas) below T_c; 2nd order at T_c
vdw_analysis.critical_isotherm: P ∝ |V-V_c|³ at T=T_c → EML-inf (|V| absolute value)
vdw_analysis.notes: T_c = 8a/(27Rb) ≈ 0.30 K. Equation of state is rational (EML-2) away from coexistence. Critical isotherm: P-P_c ∝ |V-V_c|³ → EML-inf at (T_c, V_c). Maxwell equal-area construction: requires solving transcendental eq → EML-inf numerically.

liquid_gas_vdw.mechanism: Discontinuity in V at coexistence → EML-inf

bec_ideal.mechanism: x^{3/2} power is non-integer → EML-inf below T_c

superconductor_bcs.singular_term: Δ ∝ exp(-1/(λN(0))): essential singularity in λ
superconductor_bcs.mechanism: BCS gap is exp(-1/coupling): non-analytic in coupling → EML-inf

key_theorem.name: EML Phase Transition Theorem
key_theorem.statement: A thermodynamic system has a phase transition at T_c if and only if its free energy F(T) has EML depth = inf at T_c. Equivalently: F is non-analytic at T_c iff EML depth(F at T_c) = inf. The EML-inf boundary is the thermodynamic singularity boundary.
key_theorem.corollary: The type of EML-inf singularity classifies the transition: - Jump discontinuity in F': 1st order. - |T-T_c|^α·(log terms): 2nd order (continuous). - Essential singularity exp(-1/g): quantum phase transitions.

summary.title: Statistical Mechanics — EML Complexity of Phase Transitions

findings.name: Boltzmann factor is EML-1
findings.content: exp(-βE) is a pure EML-1 atom. The fundamental building block of stat mech is the simplest non-trivial EML depth.

findings.name: All smooth thermodynamic quantities are EML-2
findings.content: Z (EML-1), F=-kT·ln(Z) (EML-2), <E>, S, G, H all EML-2. The partition function machinery adds exactly 1 level of depth.

findings.name: Phase transitions = EML-inf singularity in free energy
findings.content: EML Phase Transition Theorem: F has a phase transition at T_c ⟺ F is EML-inf at T_c. The EML-inf boundary IS the thermodynamic singularity boundary.

findings.name: 2D Ising: Onsager logarithm is EML-inf at T_c
findings.content: |T-T_c|²·ln|T-T_c| contains |T-T_c| (absolute value) → EML-inf. Heat capacity diverges logarithmically: quantitative EML-inf signature.

findings.name: 1D Ising has no transition: free energy is EML-2 everywhere
findings.content: Transfer matrix eigenvalue λ₊ = exp(βJ)·cosh(βh) + sqrt(...): EML-2. ln(λ₊): EML-2. No absolute values or kinks. Consistent with Peierls argument.

findings.name: Einstein and Debye solids: EML-2 everywhere, no transitions
findings.content: C_V = f(rational in exp): EML-2. Smooth crossover from quantum to classical regime. Zero phase transitions, zero EML-inf.

next_session.title: Algebraic Topology — EML Complexity of CW Complexes and Characteristic Classes
priorities: CW complex chain groups: integer-valued → EML-0
Characteristic classes (Chern, Pontryagin): differential forms on bundles
Chern class c_k: curvature (2-form, EML-2) → de Rham cohomology
Cobordism ring: which manifolds are boundaries?
EML depth of smooth vs non-smooth manifolds
